data.title: Kongsberg Automotive Opens New Manufacturing Facility in India
data.description:
Kongsberg Automotive opened its new plant in Faridabad, India, relocating from its previous 5,946-square-meter facility in the same area.

The Norwegian automotive parts manufacturer's new 6,947-square-meter facility is intended to boost its manufacturing capabilities and footprint in India, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Iron ore slides on heightened Sino-US trade tension, China stimulus uncertainty
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices slid on Wednesday, as a further escalation of trade tension between China and the United States heightened fears on demand outlook while doubts mounted on stimulus prospects following a batch of upbeat Chinese data.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) recouped some earlier losses to end daytime trade 0.14% lower at 708 yuan a metric ton.

The benchmark May iron ore (SZZFK5) on the Singapore Exchange slipped 1.28% to $97.45 a ton, as of 0717 GMT.

China's economy grew 5.4% year-on-year in the first quarter, data showed on Wednesday, surpassing estimates, underpinned by solid consumption and industrial output.

Also, China's new home prices were unchanged in March from the prior month, signaling a slight improvement from February, when prices fell 0.1% month-on-month.

Hopes that Beijing will unveil aggressive stimulus to counter U.S. tariff shocks to achieve its annual growth target somewhat dimmed, putting downward pressure on the ferrous market.

The weakness in prices came despite signs of lower supply and resilient demand.

Rio Tinto , reported its lowest first-quarter iron ore shipments since 2019 and warned that more weather disruptions could lead to a 2025 forecast miss.

Brazilian miner Vale produced 67.7 million metric tons of iron ore in the first quarter of 2025, down 4.5% from a year earlier.

China's crude steel output in March climbed 4.6% from the prior year, incentivised by higher margins and robust exports.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE were mixed, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! down 0.77% and coke (DCJcv1) up 0.26%, respectively.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange lost ground. Rebar fell 1.06%, hot-rolled coil shed 1.05%, wire rod (SWRcv1) lost 0.72% and stainless steel nudged down 0.08%.

"Steel demand has shown signs of softening from last week; the impact of trade tensions on exports will probably not show up until May," said Zhuo Quiqiu, an analyst at broker Jinrui Futures.

"Eyes are all on potential stimulus, the timing and scale."

data.title: PRL Global to Begin Due Diligence on Centrex Acquisition
data.description:
PRL Global has been granted a 45-day exclusive due diligence period for the potential acquisition of Centrex and its unit Agriflex, according to a Wednesday filing with the Australian bourse.

Final terms of the proposed acquisition will be determined pending completion of due diligence, the filing said.

data.title: Global banks cut China growth forecasts as trade war deepens
data.description:
Global investment banks are lowering their projections for China's economic growth this year as U.S. President Donald Trump's aggressive tariffs are expected to take a heavy toll on the world's second-largest economy.

Some of the banks had upgraded their forecasts for China just a month ago, encouraged by signs of improvement in the sputtering economy in the first two months of the year.

Sino-U.S. trade tensions have intensified after Trump announced reciprocal tariffs on April 2, leading to tit-for-tat duties on each other's goods. By April 11, China was all but under a U.S. trade embargo as tariffs rose to 145%.

China's full-year gross domestic product growth is predicted to hit 4.5% in 2025, compared with last year's 5.0% pace, according to a Reuters poll, falling short of the official target of around 5.0%.

China's first-quarter GDP beat expectations, official data showed on Wednesday, growing 5.4% year-on-year. Analysts had expected a 5.1% increase.

Here is a summary of some forecasts for the China's GDP.

NEW (PREVIOUS)

INVESTMENT HOUSE

2025

2026

CITI

4.2% (4.7%)

GOLDMAN SACHS

4% (4.5%)

3.5% (4%)

UBS

3.4% (4%)

3% (3%)

MORGAN STANLEY

4.2% (4.5%)

ANZ

4.2% (4.8%)

4.3% (4.5%)

SOCIETE GENERALE

4% (4.7%)

4% (4.5%)

NOMURA

4% (4.5%)

COMMERZBANK

3.8% (4.3%)

3.6% (4%)

KEY QUOTES:

** UBS

"Under our current new baseline assumptions, we estimate tariff hikes this year to pose a more than two-percentage-point drag on China's GDP growth. We expect China's exports to the U.S. to fall by 2/3 in the coming quarters and its overall exports to fall by 10% in USD terms in 2025, the latter also takes into account slower U.S. and global growth.

While tariff exemptions will likely reduce the inflationary pressure somewhat in the U.S., we expect they are unlikely to affect importers' desire to find alternatives to imports from China. Therefore, we expect continued negative impact of the tariff hikes on China's exports in 2026."

** CITI

"We see little scope for a deal between the U.S. and China after recent escalations.

Domestic policies could focus more on demand expansion. We expect additional funding of 1 to 1.5 trillion yuan ($204.81 billion) while policy implementation accelerates. The People's Bank of China (PBOC) could cut policy rates by 40 basis points and reserve requirement ratio (RRR) by 100 basis points. Policy constraints such as the exchange rate and debt management could stay, however. With prolonged elevated uncertainties, policymakers could choose to keep more powder dry."

** GOLDMAN SACHS

"Recent events have underscored the speed with which President Trump can alter tariff rates, while also highlighting the likelihood that high tariffs on Chinese goods will persist.

We estimate that 10 to 20 million workers in China may be exposed to U.S.-bound exports. The combination of extremely high U.S. tariffs, sharply declining exports to the U.S., and a slowing global economy is expected to generate substantial pressures on the Chinese economy and labour market."

** MORGAN STANLEY

"Policymakers are likely to front-load the 2 trillion yuan stimulus package announced at the National People's Congress (NPC) in the second quarter. Possible measures include modest monetary easing, front-loaded issuance and deployment of local construction bonds, a ramp-up of the consumer goods trade-in program, launch of national fertility subsidies, and faster housing inventory digestion.

With the bulk of the NPC budget being rolled out, Beijing could introduce a 1-1.5 trillion yuan supplementary fiscal package in the second half of 2025. This could include expanded quotas for trade-in subsidies, targeted support for services consumption, and enhanced infrastructure and tech investment support. Altogether, we expect the policy stimulus to translate into an additional 60-basis-point boost to growth."

** ANZ

"Despite a solid Q1 GDP at 5.4%, we see Q2 activity is immediately affected by the uncertainty in US tariff policy. Our view is that the tariff shock is caused by the unpredictability rather than the tariff itself. President Trump’s announcements have affected business sentiment and activity."

** SOCIETE GENERALE

"China's total exports could collapse by over 10% – similar to in the GFC in 2008-09. Of course, there is also an upside case of a 'pause' where domestic pressure builds in the US, forcing Trump to make a course correction.

"We assume the additional policy stimulus will reach 2.5% of GDP (RMB 3.5 trillion), spread out over a few quarters. This is entirely plausible given this is a once-in-a-century trade shock. Beyond measures to stabilise the economy in the short term, one important consensus among policymakers is that China needs to rebalance its economic model towards consumption, and the trade war is certainly going to accelerate progress on that."

**NOMURA

"Though we expect Beijing to significantly step up its efforts to replace the loss of exports to the U.S. with domestic demand, this will likely be quite challenging. China's economy faces two material drags simultaneously: the ongoing property fallout internally and the unprecedented U.S.-China trade war externally. The rapidly worsening U.S.-China trade war might also deal a further blow to the still-struggling property sector, including property markets in tier-one cities, which have been showing some signs of stabilization.

"U.S. and China are stuck in an unprecedented, and expensive, game of chicken, and it seems both sides are unwilling to back down. We expect tensions between these two mega economies to worsen significantly, especially as China has been making large strides in high-tech sectors, including AI and robotics."

** COMMERZBANK

"Beijing is committed to stronger policy stimulus this year, with strengthening consumption being the number one priority. The stimulus measures are expected to be frontloaded to support consumption, the property sector, technology innovation, and trade. The Politburo, a top decision-making body of the ruling Communist Party, is expected to hold a meeting later this month. More clues will likely be provided as well as more concrete policy steps will probably be announced after the meeting."

($1 = 7.3239 Chinese yuan)

data.title: China Shares Mixed as Tariffs Cloud Better-Than-Expected Q1 Performance; Kente Catalysts Soars 226% on Debut
data.description:
Chinese shares offered a mixed picture on Wednesday, despite the release of robust first-quarter economic growth figures.

The Shanghai Composite Index added 0.3%, or 8.34 points, to end at 3,276.00. The Shenzhen Component Index fell 0.9%, or 83.37 points, to 9,774.73.

The mixed performance came after the National Bureau of Statistics reported that China's GDP grew 5.4% year-on-year in the first quarter of 2025, beating the 5.1% growth forecast of Reuters-polled analysts.

Further positive economic indicators included strong growth in retail sales, which increased by 5.9%, and industrial output, which saw a 7.7% rise.

However, despite the encouraging economic data, analysts cautioned that the outlook could be clouded by the potential impact of US tariffs on Chinese goods.

In corporate news, Kente Catalysts soared 226% at the close of its first trading day on the Shanghai bourse. Its share price tripled from its initial public offering of 15.00 yuan per share.

Elsewhere, OFILM Group jumped 4.6% after announcing plans to acquire 28.2% of Oufeiguang Microelectronics (Nanchang) through share issuance and cash.

data.title: Nexus Starts Drilling at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Nexus Minerals started a 10,000-metre air core drilling program at its Wallbrook Gold Project, in Western Australia, according to a Wednesday report by the Australian bourse.

Drilling is expected to continue through April and May, with assay results anticipated thereafter, the filing said.

Shares of the company rose 3% at market close.

data.title: India's Trade Deficit Widens as Imports Jump
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

India's trade deficit expanded in March, with imports rising more sharply than exports as uncertainty over tariffs rippled across global trade.

The merchandise trade deficit stood at $21.54 billion in March, according to data published Tuesday by the country's commerce ministry. That compared with the $15.34 billion recorded in the same month a year earlier.

In February, India recorded a trade deficit of $14.05 billion.

Exports for March ticked up 0.7% to $41.97 billion, while imports rose 11% to $63.51 billion, the figures showed.

The country could face a further deficit widening ahead, with U.S. President Trump announcing, and then pausing, a 26% reciprocal tariff on Indian goods in April. Talks between both sides are underway.

Barclays economists said the higher-than-usual rise in imports in March was due in part to seasonal factors, and led by oil, gold and electronic goods. Potential front-loading ahead of tariffs was likely also in play.

Amid escalating trade tensions between the U.S. and China, India's Ministry of Commerce is monitoring the risk of imports surging and the diversion of Chinese factory goods and farm produce from the U.S., setting up a panel to watch for potential dumping, the economists said.

For the financial year ended March, exports edged up 0.1% compared to a year earlier. The main drivers included coffee, electronic goods and drugs & pharmaceuticals, among others, the ministry said.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Higher U.K. Inflation in April Is Nailed OnMarket Talk
data.description: The drop in U.K. inflation in March is only a temporary reprieve as a hefty increase is nailed on for April, Suren Thiru at the Institute of Chartered Accountants says. Rising energy bills and surging business costs, including national-insurance taxes, will likely lift inflation to around 3.5% from March's 2.6%, he says in a note. While services inflation remains strong, a loosening labor market and flagging economy could eventually put it on a downward trajectory, despite April's minimum-wage increase, Thiru says. U.S. tariffs could raise costs for businesses, but prices for goods from countries such as China could fall. The Bank of England may want to get a better handle of the impact of this before lowering interest rates in May, he argues. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese Shares Ended Mixed; Semiconductor Stocks GainMarket Talk
data.description: Chinese shares ended mixed. The Shanghai Composite Index gained 0.3%, the Shenzhen Composite Index fell 1.1% and the ChiNext Price Index declined by 1.2%. Any developments in the trade tensions between U.S. and China remain in focus. Among the major stocks, semiconductor companies led the gains. Hygon Information Technology added 2.7% and SMIC rose 1.8% after Nvidia said it expected to take up a charge of about US$5.5 billion associated with its AI H20 chips for the first quarter. Decliners included BYD, which dropped 2.2% and CATL, which closed 1.4% lower. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Viscaria's environmental permit gains legal force
data.description:
The Supreme Court of Sweden today announced that it will not grant leave to appeal Viscaria's environmental permit. Today's decision means that Viscaria's environmental permit can no longer be appealed and thus gains legal force.

“We are very pleased with the court's decision. With this, we have passed the last regulatory milestone, and we can continue with full force the preparations for the reopening of the Viscaria copper mine. We now have all the permits in place to start the construction of the industrial area including the enrichment plant, and to start mining in the mine. Today's announcement is a major step towards our vision of becoming a major contributor to the growing global demand for sustainably produced copper,” says Jörgen Olsson, CEO of Viscaria.

The background

The Land and Environment Court's judgment of May 6, 2024 was appealed by Gabna Sami village, three private individuals and Viscaria to the Land and Environment Court of Appeal. On November 6, 2024, the Land and Environment Court of Appeal announced that the court does not grant leave to appeal. This decision was appealed on December 4, 2024 by Gabna Sami village to the Supreme Court, which today announced that the court does not grant leave to appeal.

data.title: M.O.B.A. Network Enhances Video Creator Services – Positioning for Growth and Stronger Margins
data.description:
Stockholm, April 16, 2025M.O.B.A. Network is actively evolving its strategy in response to changing user preferences and market dynamics, marking an important shift toward promising new growth areas, particularly within video and fintech. By rapidly adapting and prioritizing user- and creator-centric solutions, the company has identified significant opportunities to expand and diversify its revenue streams.

Union For Gamers, M.O.B.A. Network’s Multi-Channel Network (MCN) for video creators, faced volatility during 2023 and early 2024, experiencing a decrease in associated creators and significant fluctuations in views per creator. Additionally, declining revenue per view created challenging conditions for this segment. This volatility was felt broadly across the creator services landscape, with global macro-economic factors impacting consumer confidence and leading to knock-on effects across the industry, as well as a lull in trending video game releases reducing content creation opportunities among our existing creators.

In response, M.O.B.A. Network embarked on intensive development efforts during fall 2023 to strengthen its position in the video segment. A strategic partnership with Mediacube was established to revitalize Union For Gamers and enhance the overall offerings to creators. We are pleased to report that these efforts are now delivering positive results. The video segment has demonstrated consistent growth over the past three quarters with increased video views, overall watch time, and number of associated creators, validating our strategic approach. 

A particularly encouraging development has been our investment in complementary services for creators, notably within fintech. By offering industry-leading payment solutions tailored to content creators, including more ways to withdraw earnings, get paid faster, and receive advance funding, we have significantly enhanced the value proposition for our creators, while also appealing to a wider user base and creating profitable new revenue streams. Fintech services offer attractive margins and considerable future potential.

Although challenges remain, M.O.B.A. Network is leveraging valuable lessons from the past year. With a clear emphasis on strategic partnerships and innovative business models that deliver enduring value to our creators, users, and shareholders.

data.title: The CAC 40 Index Opens 0.71% Lower
data.description:
In Paris, the CAC 40 Index is dropping 52 points.

Leading the losses are Stellantis NV (-3.68%), Edenred (-3.62%) and Publicis (-2.66%).

data.title: ABB India gains; Motilal Oswal sees higher inflows in few quarters
data.description:
** ABB India rises 2.6% to 5,381 rupees

** Stock set for third straight session of gains since releasing annual report on April 9, gaining 7% during the period; April 10 and April 14 were market holidays

** Motilal Oswal says engineering products maker's FY25 annual report highlights focus on high-growth segments and deeper penetration in tier-II cities

** Expect inflows and project ramp-ups after a few quarters

** Re-iterates "buy", with PT of 6,700 rupees

data.title: ASML Fiscal Q1 Earnings, Sales Rise; Issues Q2 Sales Guidance
data.description:
ASML Holding reported Wednesday fiscal Q1 earnings of 6 euros ($6.82) per diluted share, up from 3.11 euros a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 5.74 euros.

Net sales for the quarter ended March 30 were 7.74 billion euros, up from 5.29 billion euros a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected 7.77 billion euros.

The company expects Q2 net sales to range from 7.2 billion euros to 7.7 billion euros. Analysts polled by FactSet expect 7.80 billion euros.

The company maintained its 2025 sales guidance of between 30 billion euros and 35 billion euros. Analysts polled by FactSet are expecting 32.49 billion euros.

data.title: The Calmer Co. International's Instant Kava Product to be Ranged across Woolworth Supermarkets
data.description:
The Calmer Co. International said its Fiji Kava 50 grams Instant Kava product will be ranged across the Woolsworth Group supermarket network from June, according to a Wednesday Australian bourse filing.

The firm already has existing distribution in more than 800 Coles Group supermarkets and Quickstop convenience locations.

Calmer's shares jumped 25% on market close on Wednesday.

data.title: AXA'S private equity firm CAPZA in talks for majority stake in Mecadaq, a strategic player in aerospace
data.description:
CAPZA/MECADAQ:

* CAPZA, a leading private investment firm for European SMEs, announces it has entered exclusive negotiations to buy a majority stake in Mecadaq, alongside its President, Julien Dubecq, and its management team, who will significantly reinvest in the transaction.

* Mecadaq specializes in the machining of complex, high value-added detailed parts for the civil and defense aerospace sectors.

* With three production sites in France and one in the United States, the Group supports major global OEMs — including Airbus, Boeing, Dassault, Safran, Thalès — on critical programs such as A320, B737 and Rafale.

* CAPZA is part of AXA IM Alts .

data.title: Global Growth Expectations Hit 30-Year Low, BofA SurveyMarket Talk
data.description: U.S. tariffs drive global growth expectations to their lowest in 30 years, Bank of America's April global fund manager survey finds. A net 82% of respondents say the global economy is set to weaken, marking a 30-year high, BofA says in a press release accompanying the survey. Recession is likely according to 42% of respondents, while the probability of a 'hard landing'--falling inflation combined with a significant economic slowdown or recession--surges to 49% from 11% in March. Expectations of a 'soft landing'--where inflation slows without the economy slowing too much--falls to 37% from 64%, the survey says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: The DAX Index Opens 0.69% Lower

Biggest rises came from Sartorius (1.71%), E.ON (1.04%) and RWE (0.42%).

data.title: Stocks fall on tariff woes, baht near 6-month high
data.description:
Key points:
  • MSCI emerging Asia stocks fall
  • Stocks in Malaysia and the Philippines fall
  • Thai baht rallies to near 6-month high
  • Other Asian currencies tick higher

Stock markets in emerging Asia fell on Wednesday, snapping a four-day winning streak, as U.S. curbs on some Nvidia chip exports to China amplified trade tensions between the top economies.

Among currencies, the Thai baht surged as much as 33.22 per U.S. dollar for the first time since October 21, 2024, driven by a softer greenback, record high gold prices and China's better-than-expected economic growth in the first quarter.

Thailand is a major gold trading hub in the region, and its currency is closely linked to the commodity's price.

"The outperformance in the baht should be contributed by surging gold prices," said Poon Panichpibool, a markets strategist at Krung Thai Bank.

Analysts, however, remain cautious about short-term rallies in the baht as a correction in gold prices could pressure the currency. An escalation in the U.S.-China trade war and the knock-on effect of a weakening yuan could also dampen other emerging Asian currencies.

Panichpibool said the baht remained under pressure due to a weak tourism season, ongoing trade tensions and dividend repatriation by foreign investors.

In China, solid first-quarter growth data did little to boost domestic assets, with the onshore yuan slipping marginally and stocks falling.

China is Southeast Asia's biggest trading partner and a top investment source whose fortunes dictate growth in the developing region.

Most other emerging currencies appreciated against a subdued dollar , with the greenback clinging to mid-July lows touched last week against a basket of its peers.

The Singapore dollar and the Philippine peso firmed 0.3%, while Malaysia's ringgit moved in a tight range around its one-week high. Indonesia's rupiah ticked lower to 16,838 a dollar.

An MSCI index of world emerging market currencies (.MIEM00000CUS) was slightly lower, largely weighed down by Latin American currencies that weakened against the dollar overnight. A gauge of those currencies (.MILA00000CUS) slipped 0.3%.

In equities, the MSCI index of Asian emerging market stocks (.MIMS00000PUS) fell as much as 1.8% to snap a four-day streak. A gauge of ASEAN stocks (.MISU00000PUS) - dominated by Southeast Asian firms - also fell slightly.

Tariff volleys between the United States and China have traders fretting about global economic growth, with President Donald Trump's ever-changing policies adding to the uncertainty.

"Choppiness in markets looks to continue as the trade negotiations come underway together with the risk of more U.S. trade restriction announcements," analysts at Maybank said.

Equity benchmarks in Malaysia and the Philippines declined up to 1%, while those in Thailand and Indonesia edged lower. Singapore's FTSE Straits Times index bucked the trend, rising marginally.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Indonesia's 10-year benchmark bond yield (ID10YT=RR) slips to 6.992%

** Thailand plans to import more U.S. LNG over next 5 years, says minister

** China unexpectedly anoints new trade negotiator amid U.S. tariff war

** Indonesia to propose $10 billion US energy imports increase in tariff talks

data.title: Austria Inflation Rate Confirmed at 2.9%
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Austria slowed to 2.9% in March 2025 from 3.2% in the previous month, matching preliminary estimates and approaching the ECB’s 2% stability target.

Prices moderated mainly for clothing and footwear (0.2% vs 1.8% in February), recreation and culture (2% vs 3.3%), and restaurants and hotels (5.8% vs 6%).

Additionally, costs declined for furnishings, household equipment and maintenance (-0.9% vs -0.7%) and transport (-0.8% vs 0.8%).

Meanwhile, inflation increased further for food and non-alcoholic beverages (3.1% vs 2.6%) and alcoholic beverages and tobacco (3.7% vs 1.4%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 0.2% in March, following a 0.6% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: China Foreign Ministry: Urge US to stop threats and blackmail
data.description:
• Urge US to stop threats and blackmail.
  • If US wants to solve issues through dialogue, it should stop exerting maximum pressure.

data.title: NCC Lands SEK150 Million University Building Redevelopment Project in Sweden
data.description:
NCC (NCC-B.ST, NCC-A.ST) secured a turnkey project worth 150 million kronor from property owner Hemsö to redevelop Mälardalen University's "S" building in Sweden.

The Swedish construction company will convert the building into the university's administrative offices, according to a Wednesday release. Construction is slated to start in May 2025, with completion expected by the end of 2026.

data.title: Barratt Redrow's Update Bland But Welcome Given Economic BackdropMarket Talk
data.description: Barratt Redrow's update is dull and that is probably a good thing given the global market turmoil backdrop, Jefferies analysts say in a note. The company backed completions guidance for FY 2025,with its order book benefiting from house-price inflation as well as mix, Jefferies says. Still, the FTSE 100 listed house builder's slightly improving selling rates might fail to excite, with lower affordable sales continuing to constrain order book progression, Jefferies says. "Growth in the value of the private order book and evidence of house-price inflation in the order book should reassure of the robustness of customer demand," Jefferies says. Shares are up 4.40 pence, or 1%, at 432.10 pence. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)
